cards.prompt: The same lambda x => x > 2: a Func is opaque (only del(5)=True is observable) while Expression<Func<int,bool>> captures a navigable tree whose Body.NodeType is GreaterThan and whose right leaf is Constant 2, so the output is del:True tree.NodeType:GreaterThan right:2
cards.code: using System; using System.Linq.Expressions; Func<int,bool> del = x => x > 2; Expression<Func<int,bool>> tree = x => x > 2; var b=(BinaryExpression)tree.Body; Console.WriteLine($"del:{del(5)} tree.NodeType:{tree.Body.NodeType} right:{((ConstantExpression)b.Right).Value}");

cards.prompt: A mini provider reads the tree of x => x > 2: Left.Name is the column x, NodeType is the operator GreaterThan, Right.Value is the value 2, and it assembles WHERE x GreaterThan 2 without executing the lambda
cards.code: using System; using System.Linq.Expressions; Expression<Func<int,bool>> tree = x => x > 2; var b=(BinaryExpression)tree.Body; var col=((ParameterExpression)b.Left).Name; var op=b.NodeType; var val=((ConstantExpression)b.Right).Value; Console.WriteLine($"WHERE {col} {op} {val}");

cards.prompt: Expression<Func<int,bool>> for x => x > 2 exposes the full structure a provider needs: root NodeType Lambda, single parameter x, body NodeType GreaterThan, ReturnType.Name Boolean, so the output is Lambda|x|GreaterThan|Boolean
cards.code: using System; using System.Linq.Expressions; Expression<Func<int,bool>> tree = x => x > 2; Console.WriteLine($"{tree.NodeType}|{tree.Parameters[0].Name}|{tree.Body.NodeType}|{tree.ReturnType.Name}");
